zh.lifeSummary: 當Salome of Greater Poland於1230年出生在波蘭時，她的父親Wladyslaw Odonic40歲，母親Jadwiga43歲。她於1249年與Konrad I of Glogów Duke of Glogów結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和2個女兒的父母。她去世於1267年4月享年37歲。

nameMeanings.name: Salome
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of an unrecorded Aramaic name, akin to the Hebrew word shalom ‘peace’. It was common at the time of Christ, and was borne by one of the women who were at his tomb and witnessed the Resurrection (Mark 16:1–8). This would normally have led to its common use as a Christian name, and it is indeed found as such in medieval times. However, according to the Jewish historian Josephus, it was also the name of King Herod's stepdaughter, the daughter of Queen Herodias. In the Bible, a daughter of Herodias, generally identified as this Salome, danced for Herod and so pleased him that he offered to give her anything she wanted. Prompted by her mother, she asked for (and got) the head of John the Baptist, who was in one of Herod's prisons (Mark 6:17–28). This story so gripped medieval imagination that the name Salome became more or less taboo until the end of the 19th century, when Oscar Wilde wrote a play about her and some unconventional souls began to choose the name for their daughters.

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of Oswald or any of the various other names beginning with Os-.

nameMeanings.name: Greater
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Americanized form of South German   Grieser   or   Griesser  .

variants.name: Grieser
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Fritz, Dieter, Gerhard.
topographic name for someone living on a sandy site, from Middle High German griez ‘sand’ + -er suffix denoting an inhabitant.

nameMeanings.name: Poland
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old French personal name of Germanic (Frankish) origin, from hrōd ‘fame’ + land ‘land, territory’. This was adopted by the Normans and introduced by them to Britain. In Old French literature it is borne by a legendary Frankish hero, a vassal of Charlemagne, whose exploits are told in the Chanson de Roland. The subject of the poem is Roland's death at the Battle of Roncesvalles in the Pyrenees in 778 , while protecting the rearguard of the Frankish army on its retreat from Spain. Roland is depicted in literature and legend as headstrong and impulsive. His devoted friendship with the prudent Oliver is also legendary.

variants.name: Rowland
meanings: Variant spelling of   Roland  , or a transferred use of the surname derived from that given name in the Middle Ages.

en.lifeSummary: When Salome of Greater Poland was born in 1230, in Poland, her father, Wladyslaw Odonic, was 40 and her mother, Jadwiga, was 43. She married Konrad I of Glogów Duke of Glogów in 1249. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 2 daughters. She died in April 1267, at the age of 37.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Salome of Greater Poland est née en 1230, à Pologne, son père, Wladyslaw Odonic, avait 40 ans et sa mère, Jadwiga, avait 43 ans. Elle a épousé Konrad I of Glogów Duke of Glogów en 1249. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 2 filles. Elle est décédée en avril 1267, à l’âge de 37 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Salome of Greater Poland 1230 in Polen geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Wladyslaw Odonic 40 und ihre Mutter Jadwiga 43. Sie hat 1249 Konrad I of Glogów Duke of Glogów geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Sie starb im April 1267 im Alter von 37 Jahren.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Salome of Greater Poland è nata il nel 1230 nel Polonia, sua padre, Wladyslaw Odonic, aveva 40 anni e sua madre, Jadwiga, aveva 43 anni. Lei sposato Konrad I of Glogów Duke of Glogów nel 1249. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli e 2 figlie. Morì nel aprile 1267, all'età di 37 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Salome of Greater Polandが1230年にポーランドで生まれた時，父Wladyslaw Odonicは40歳，母Jadwigaは43歳であった。1249年にKonrad I of Glogów Duke of Glogówと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼女は1267年4月に37歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Salome of Greater Poland(이)가 1230년 폴란드에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Wladyslaw Odonic(은)는 40세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Jadwiga(은)는 43세였다. 그녀는 1249년 Konrad I of Glogów Duke of Glogów(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1267년 4월 37세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Salome of Greater Poland nasceu em 1230, em Polônia, seu pai, Wladyslaw Odonic, tinha 40 anos e sua mãe, Jadwiga, tinha 43 anos. Ela casou-se com Konrad I of Glogów Duke of Glogów em 1249. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos e 2 filhas. Ela faleceu em abril de 1267, com 37 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Salome of Greater Poland родилась в 1230 г. в местности Польша, её отцу по имени Wladyslaw Odonic было 40 лет, а матери, которую звали Jadwiga, 43. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Konrad I of Glogów Duke of Glogów в 1249 г. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 2 дочери. Она умерла апрель 1267 г. в возрасте 37 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Salome of Greater Poland nació en 1230, en Polonia, su papá, Wladyslaw Odonic, tenía 40 años y su mamá, Jadwiga, tenía 43 años. Se casó con Konrad I of Glogów Duke of Glogów en 1249. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos y 2 hijas. Murió en abril de 1267, a la edad de 37 años.
